rankings.title: Minimum Standards or Minimum Supply? MEES Regulation and the Rental Housing Market
conditions: a defensible rental-status definition
a pre-specified supply measure that is not mechanically driven by EPC re-lodgements
RDD manipulation/bunching handled explicitly as part of the mechanism rather than ignored

rankings.title: Do Energy Labels Move Markets? Multi-Cutoff RDD Evidence from English Property Transactions
Does Rate Relief Save the High Street? Evidence from the £12,000 Threshold
conditions: validating trading-location measures—e.g.
business register/VAT/PAYE or local vacancy datasets
demonstrating limited sorting at £12k or re-framing as a manipulation-inclusive estimand

rankings.title: Building on Borrowed Time: Flood Re Eligibility and Property Values
Priced Out of Clean Air? Property Value Effects of London's ULEZ Boundary
Idea 5: Minimum Standards or Minimum Supply? MEES Regulation and the Rental Housing Market
Idea 1: Do Energy Labels Move Markets? Multi-Cutoff RDD Evidence from English Property Transactions
conditions: passing McCrary density tests at the informational thresholds
or using a "donut RDD" to exclude manipulated observations

rankings.title: Idea 4: Building on Borrowed Time: Flood Re Eligibility and Property Values
conditions: implementing a spatial placebo to control for the 2008/2009 housing market crash

rankings.title: Idea 2: Does Rate Relief Save the High Street? Evidence from the £12,000 Threshold
Idea 3: Priced Out of Clean Air? Property Value Effects of London's ULEZ Boundary
Idea 1: Do Energy Labels Move Markets? Multi-Cutoff RDD Evidence from English Property Transactions
conditions: strong McCrary across cutoffs
pre-specify crisis interaction in pre-analysis plan

rankings.title: Idea 5: Minimum Standards or Minimum Supply? MEES Regulation and the Rental Housing Market
conditions: differentiation from Idea 1 via rental-specific supply data
RW multiple-testing
